sentence: Hoje o tempo não está nada bom.
ipa: ˈɔʒɨ ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Inherited EP reduction to [ɨ]/[ɐ] and coda [ʃ] in 'está' per mateus_dandrade2000; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in monosyllabic 'não' is retained, unlike the plural -ões/-ães reduction per segura2013; 'Hoje o' vowel liaison.

sentence: Este quilo de peixe custa seis euros.
ipa: ˈeʃtɨ ˈkiʎu ˈdɨ ˈpejʃɨ ˈkuʃtɐ ˈsejz ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: This kilo of fish costs six euros.
notes: Diagnostic Madeiran intervocalic /l/ after /i/ palatalises: 'quilo' [ˈkiʎu] per segura2013, native-speaker attested (quilómetro -> 'quilhómetro') per pwl_madeira; coda [ʃ] and 'ei' in 'peixe' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'seis euros' regressive voicing.

sentence: A reunião é às três horas na vila.
ipa: ˈɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ ˈaʃ ˈtɾez ˈɔɾɐʒ ˈnɐ ˈviʎɐ
gloss_en: The meeting is at three o'clock in the town.
notes: Palatalised 'vila' [ˈviʎɐ] (intervocalic /l/ after /i/) per segura2013, confirmed per pwl_madeira; strong initial [ʁ] in 'reunião' and coda [ʃ] in 'às'/'três' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'três horas' voicing junction.

sentence: O meu irmão mora perto da levada.
ipa: ˈo ˈmew iˈɾmɐ̃w̃ ˈmɔɾɐ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdɐ lɨˈvadɐ
gloss_en: My brother lives near the levada.
notes: Madeiran keeps the /v/ ~ /b/ contrast (no betacism): 'levada' [lɨˈvadɐ] stays [v] per mateus_dandrade2000; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'irmão' retained per segura2013; 'meu irmão' liaison.

sentence: Segue pela vereda até à ribeira.
ipa: ˈsɨɡɨ ˈpɛlɐ vɨˈɾɛdɐ ɐˈtɛ ˈa ʁiˈbejɾɐ
gloss_en: Follow the path down to the stream.
notes: Strong initial [ʁ] in 'ribeira' and 'ei' diphthong per mateus_dandrade2000; open-mid [ɛ] in 'vereda'/'até'; 'até à' vowel elision; imperative register per segura2013.

sentence: A espetada e o milho frito estavam bons.
ipa: ˈɐ eʃpɨˈtadɐ ˈe ˈo ˈmiʎu ˈfɾitu eˈʃtavɐ̃ ˈbõʃ
gloss_en: The beef skewer and the fried corn were good.
notes: Madeiran regional dish lexicon (espetada, milho frito) attested per pwl_madeira; palatal [ʎ] in the 'lh' of 'milho' and coda [ʃ] in 'espetada'/'bons' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'espetada e o' liaisons.

sentence: Onde fica a casa do teu avô?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈfikɐ ˈɐ ˈkazɐ ˈdu ˈtew ɐˈvo
gloss_en: Where is your grandfather's house?
notes: Nasal [õ] in 'onde' and close-mid [o] in 'avô' per mateus_dandrade2000; reduced pretonics; 'fica a' vowel liaison; interrogative frame per segura2013.

sentence: Quantos limões precisas para o bolo?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tuʒ liˈmõns pɾɨˈsizɐʃ ˈpaɾɐ ˈo ˈbɔlu
gloss_en: How many lemons do you need for the cake?
notes: Salient Madeiran nasal-diphthong reduction -ões -> [õns]: 'limões' [liˈmõns] per segura2013, native-speaker attested (leões -> 'leôns') per pwl_madeira; 'quantos limões' voicing junction.

sentence: Se pudesse, comprava aquela quinta.
ipa: ˈsɨ puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾavɐ ɐˈkɛlɐ ˈkĩtɐ
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that farmstead.
notes: Reduced pretonics and coda [ʃ] in 'pudesse', nasal [ĩ] in 'quinta' per mateus_dandrade2000; conditional-imperfect frame per segura2013; 'comprava aquela' liaison.

sentence: Comprámos pão e mel na loja da vila.
ipa: kõˈpɾamuʃ ˈpɐ̃w̃ ˈe ˈmɛɫ ˈnɐ ˈlɔʒɐ ˈdɐ ˈviʎɐ
gloss_en: We bought bread and honey in the town.
notes: Palatalised 'vila' [ˈviʎɐ] per segura2013 / pwl_madeira; velarised coda [ɫ] in 'mel' and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'pão' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'pão e' liaison.

sentence: Amanhã vamos de barco até ao Porto Santo.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈvamuʒ ˈdɨ ˈbaɾku ɐˈtɛ ˈɐu ˈpɔɾtu ˈsɐ̃tu
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by boat to Porto Santo.
notes: Madeiran toponym Porto Santo; palatal [ɲ] and nasal [ã] in 'amanhã' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'vamos de' regressive voicing of coda /s/ before [d] per segura2013.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares à cidade.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkwɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨz ˈa siˈdadɨ
gloss_en: Call me when you get to town.
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and [ʃ] in 'chegares' per mateus_dandrade2000; clitic imperative frame per segura2013; 'chegares à' voicing junction.

sentence: Bom dia, então como está a família?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈdiɐ ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu eˈʃta ˈɐ fɐˈmiʎiɐ
gloss_en: Good morning, so how is the family?
notes: Intervocalic /l/ after /i/ palatalises in 'família' [fɐˈmiʎiɐ] per segura2013 / pwl_madeira; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' and coda [ʃ] in 'está' per mateus_dandrade2000; greeting register.

sentence: Quem não arrisca, não petisca.
ipa: ˈkɨ̃ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ɐˈʁiʃkɐ ˈnɐ̃w̃ pɨˈtiʃkɐ
gloss_en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
notes: Strong [ʁ] in 'arrisca' and coda [ʃ] in 'arrisca'/'petisca' per mateus_dandrade2000; retained nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' per segura2013; proverb register.

sentence: O carro corre pela serra.
ipa: ˈo ˈkaʁu ˈkɔʁɨ ˈpɛlɐ ˈsɛʁɐ
gloss_en: The car runs along the ridge.
notes: Strong-R reflex [ʁ] for orthographic 'rr' in 'carro'/'corre'/'serra' per mateus_dandrade2000; open-mid [ɔ]/[ɛ] in 'corre'/'serra'.

sentence: As luzes do farol brilham no cais.
ipa: ˈɐʒ ˈluzɨʒ ˈdu fɐˈɾɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ̃ ˈnu ˈkajʃ
gloss_en: The lighthouse lights shine over the quay.
notes: Velarised coda [ɫ] in 'farol', palatal [ʎ] in 'brilham' and coda [ʃ]/[ʒ] in 'luzes'/'cais' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'As luzes' voicing junction.

sentence: Aquilo é um quilo de castanhas.
ipa: ɐˈkiʎu ˈɛ ˈũ ˈkiʎu ˈdɨ kɐˈʃtaɲɐʃ
gloss_en: That there is a kilo of chestnuts.
notes: Intervocalic /l/ after /i/ palatalises in both 'aquilo' [ɐˈkiʎu] and 'quilo' [ˈkiʎu] per segura2013 / pwl_madeira; palatal [ɲ] and coda [ʃ] in 'castanhas' per mateus_dandrade2000; 'Aquilo é' liaison.

sentence: Os leões e os corações são fortes.
ipa: ˈoʒ lɨˈõns ˈe ˈoʃ kuɾɐˈsõns ˈsɐ̃w̃ ˈfɔɾtɨʃ
gloss_en: Lions and hearts are strong.
notes: Productive -ões -> [õns] reduction in 'leões'/'corações' per segura2013, native attested per pwl_madeira, contrasted with retained monosyllabic 'são' [sɐ̃w̃]; 'leões e os' liaisons.

sentence: Os cães ladram aos capitães.
ipa: ˈoʃ ˈkɐ̃ns ˈladɾɐ̃ ˈɐuʃ kɐpiˈtɐ̃ns
gloss_en: The dogs bark at the captains.
notes: Productive -ães -> [ɐ̃ns] reduction in 'cães' [kɐ̃ns] and 'capitães' [kɐpiˈtɐ̃ns] per segura2013, native attested (cães -> 'câns') per pwl_madeira; 'ladram aos' liaison.

sentence: O touro correu pela ribeira ao meio-dia.
ipa: ˈo ˈtoɾu kuˈʁew ˈpɛlɐ ʁiˈbejɾɐ ˈɐu ˈmeju ˈdiɐ
gloss_en: The bull ran along the stream at midday.
notes: 'ou' in 'touro' and 'ei' in 'ribeira' per mateus_dandrade2000; strong [ʁ] in 'correu'/'ribeira'; 'ribeira ao' liaison; open-mid [ɛ] in 'pela' per segura2013.
